commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an authoritative session-file scan result and uses it to skip destructive memory session stale-row pruning when a full directory scan fails.

PR surface: Source +51, Tests +235. Total +286 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main returns `[]` for any session directory `readdir` failure, and full session sync prunes indexed `sessions` rows against that empty active-path set.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can add the missing QMD mock export and rerun the focused memory tests; the broader session-state policy can remain on maintainer review.

target.title: fix(memory): do not prune session index from a failed directory scan
target.body:
## Summary

A full session memory sync treats the sessions-directory listing as the source of truth for **destructive** pruning: `resolveMemorySessionSyncPlan` builds `activePaths` from `listSessionFilesForAgent(agentId)`, and `syncSessionFiles` then deletes every indexed session row (`files` + `chunks` + vectors + FTS) whose path is not in that listing.

The problem: `listSessionFilesForAgent` caught **all** `readdir` errors and returned `[]`, so a single transient sessions-dir scan failure (e.g. NFS `EIO`/`ESTALE`/timeout) was indistinguishable from a genuinely empty directory. On a full/non-targeted sync that empty listing prunes the **entire** session index, then the next successful scan has to re-read and re-embed every transcript — a self-inflicted, expensive wipe triggered by one filesystem blip.

This is latent on `main` today. It's the consumer-side hazard called out while reviewing #91081 (which fixed the *listing cache* from amplifying a failed read across callers, but the prune still trusts an empty listing).

## Approach

Make the enumeration **authoritative** for the destructive prune instead of guessing from an empty array:

- `packages/memory-host-sdk` — add `scanSessionFilesForAgent(agentId): { ok, files }`. `ok: true` means we actually read the directory (even if it held no session files); `ok: false` means the scan failed (transient error or missing dir). `listSessionFilesForAgent` now delegates to it and is **unchanged** for its other callers (still returns `[]` on failure).
- `resolveMemorySessionSyncPlan` — takes `scanOk` and returns `pruneStaleRows`, which is true **only** for a full enumeration that authoritatively read the directory.
- `syncSessionFiles` — uses `scanSessionFilesForAgent` for full enumerations and gates the stale-row prune on `pruneStaleRows`. A failed scan skips the prune (and logs a warning) and leaves the index intact for the next successful enumeration.

### Why skip on *failed*, not on *empty*

I deliberately did **not** use a blunt "empty listing ⇒ skip prune" guard. Deletions/resets archive to usage-counted `.deleted.`/`.reset.` artifacts that still appear in the listing, and `disk-budget` can physically remove the last archive — so an **authoritatively empty** directory with a non-empty index is legitimately reachable. In that case we must still prune, or deleted-session content would linger in search forever. Skipping only on a *failed* scan prevents the wipe without introducing persistent orphan rows.

## Verification

Worktree on latest `origin/main` (`e7b09fba37`).

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` (3 files): **27 passing**
  - `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts` — `scanSessionFilesForAgent` ok / authoritatively-empty / failed-scan / absent-dir; `listSessionFilesForAgent` still returns `[]` on failure.
  - `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-session-sync-state.test.ts` — `pruneStaleRows` for full-ok / failed-scan / authoritatively-empty / targeted.
  - `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.session-prune.test.ts` (new) — end-to-end: failed scan ⇒ **no** session rows deleted; authoritatively-empty dir ⇒ orphan row **is** pruned.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` + `pnpm tsgo:extensions` — clean.
- `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts` — clean (type-level published surface incl. the new export).
- `oxfmt --check` + `oxlint` on touched files — clean.
- Plugin-sdk boundary checks (`no-plugin-sdk-wildcard-reexports`, `no-src-outside-plugin-sdk`) — clean.

### Known gaps (CI/Testbox)
- `pnpm tsgo:test:extensions` reports pre-existing errors **only** in the untouched `extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts` (`config: unknown`); reproduced on clean `origin/main` with my changes stashed. My touched files add zero new type errors.
- Full `pnpm build` + `check-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs` (needs `dist/`) not run locally in the worktree — recommend CI coverage.

## Notes
- Independent of #91081 and complementary: #91081 hardens the listing cache; this hardens the prune that consumes the listing. No file conflicts beyond both branches touching `session-files.ts` (additive here).
target.author: amknight

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
